description_long: This is a roughly rectangular stone block with a weathered, uneven surface and a predominantly beige to light brown coloration. One face of the block features a detailed carved relief, depicting two stylized faces in profile flanking a central floral or symbolic motif, all framed within a rectangular border. The other sides are irregular and unadorned, showing natural stone texture with visible inclusions and some chipping. The block appears solid and heavy, with a coarse, granular surface typical of sedimentary or limestone material. The top and bottom surfaces are uneven, suggesting it may have been quarried or shaped by hand. The overall appearance suggests historical or architectural significance, possibly as part of a decorative or structural element in an urban or heritage setting.
description: A heavy, rectangular stone block with a carved relief on one face and rough, weathered surfaces on the others. The stone is beige-brown with visible inclusions and signs of age.
description_view_0: This view shows a rough, unadorned side of the stone block with a natural, uneven surface and visible granular texture.
description_view_1: This side is also unadorned, with a slightly smoother but still irregular surface and some discoloration or weathering marks.
description_view_2: The front face features a detailed carved relief with two stylized faces and a central motif, framed by a rectangular border.
description_view_3: This view displays another rough, uncarved side, similar in texture to view 0, with pronounced irregularities and chipping.
attribute_location_context: historic urban plaza or architectural site
attribute_weathering_pattern: Surface shows signs of chipping, erosion, and discoloration, especially on the uncarved sides. The carved face appears more protected but still exhibits some wear.
attribute_presence_of_markings_or_engrav: Yes, one face features a carved relief with two stylized faces and a central motif within a rectangular border.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: Likely forms part of a decorative wall or architectural feature, possibly aligned with other carved blocks.
